segments.goal: Connect matrix shape to data organization and system representation.
beats: Define matrix size m×n and explain row/column indexing notation a_{ij}.
Contrast coefficient matrix with augmented matrix.
State why dimensions control allowed operations.
voiceover: A matrix is a structured table, not a loose list of numbers.
Its dimensions tell us exactly how many equations and variables are being tracked.
segments.animation: progressive highlight of row and column indices

segments.goal: State the formal condition for two matrices to be equal.
beats: Require entry-wise equality for all i,j.
Show one mismatch entry as a counterexample.
voiceover: Matrix equality is strict: same shape and same entry in every position.
One mismatched entry is enough to invalidate equality.
segments.animation: split-screen equality check with red mismatch indicator

segments.goal: Practice identifying dimensions, entries, and legal comparisons.
beats: Read dimensions of three sample matrices.
Decide if two matrices can be compared for equality.
voiceover: Start by reading dimensions before touching any arithmetic.
Dimension mismatch immediately blocks equality checks.
segments.animation: stepwise annotation overlays on matrix entries

segments.goal: Confirm foundational matrix literacy before row operations.
beats: Checkpoint: explain entry notation without ambiguity.
Checkpoint: apply equality rule to short examples.
voiceover: If you can read dimensions and entries confidently, elimination becomes much cleaner.
These checkpoints should be automatic before moving to row operations.
